title: 2-step Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2g3b5","text":"Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR) is a method of making multiple copies of a DNA sequence, involving repeated reactions with a polymerase enzyme, which comes in different types, such as Taq-polymerase, Phusion or Q5.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6h4s1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"epgnt","text":"To run a PCR reaction, one needs a DNA template to be amplified, one set of forward and reverse primers, a polymerase enzyme as well as various buffers.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4kglh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9orq0","text":"This protocol describes the procedure to perform a two-step PCR reaction, in which the primers contain an extra non-binding overlap sequence which allows the assembly of a new sequence from a specific region of the template DNA. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"u7gc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fmmpp","text":"This is done by using two sets of primers, one which adds a non-binding overlap sequence and one other set which add a desired sequence binding to the overlap region introduced by the first set of primers.\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3ibbh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9pqje","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Set up the thermocycler program For initial denaturation 98°C for 2 minutes For 35 cycles: 98°C for 30 seconds for denaturation 30 seconds for primers annealing at temperature depending on primers and polymerase 72°C for 30 seconds per kb of DNA for elongation Final extension: 72°C for 7 minutes 4°C on hold

Set up the thermocycler program For initial denaturation 98°C for 30 seconds For 10 cycles: 98°C for 8 seconds for denaturation 20 seconds for primers annealing at temperature depending on primers and polymerase 72°C for 30 seconds per kb of DNA for elongation Final extension: 72°C for 7 minutes 4°C on hold

Set up the thermocycler program For initial denaturation 98°C for 2 minutes For 35 cycles: 98°C for 30 seconds for denaturation 30 seconds for primers annealing at temperature depending on primers and polymerase 72°C for 30 seconds per kb of DNA for elongation Final extension: 72°C for 7 minutes 4°C on hold
Prepare the master mix using the quantities specified in Materials
Control the PCR by doing an agarose gel electrophoresis (follow Gel Electrophoresis Protocol)
Load the PCR mixture in thermocycler and start program.
Prepare the master mix with the gene specific PCR product and using the quantities specified in Materials
Load the reaction mixture in the thermocycler and start the program
Control the PCR by doing an agarose gel electrophoresis (follow Gel Electrophoresis Protocol)
Purifiy the PCR product with a DNA purification kit

When the extension PCR is done, directly add in the tubes the primers for the final PCR with the quantities specified in Materials. Minimize the waiting time before starting the second reaction to keep polymerase activity high
creator: Dana Mozaffari
